module_map.responsibility: Application registry and configuration management. Tracks installed applications, validates configurations, and provides access to app-specific functionality via registry patterns.
Settings and configuration system. Provides application-level settings that control Django's behavior across all components.
Pluggable contrib applications providing common features like admin interface, auth, sessions, messages, etc. These are optional but widely-used modules that form part of Django's ecosystem.
Core functionality including handlers, exceptions, validators, cache, checks, files, and async support. This is the central execution layer of Django.
Database abstraction layer and ORM. Provides model definition, query construction, and database backend abstraction including transactions and migrations.
Event dispatch system and signal framework. Implements the observer pattern for decoupled communication between components.
Form handling and validation system. Defines field types, widgets, form classes, and data validation logic.
HTTP layer providing request/response objects, middleware infrastructure, and content handling. Represents the HTTP protocol abstraction layer.
Request/response middleware stack and built-in middleware components (CSRF, caching, compression, security, etc.). Defines the pipeline for processing requests through multiple transformation layers.
Template engine and rendering system. Provides template language parsing, tag libraries, context management, and backend abstraction.
URL routing and dispatch system. Maps URL patterns to views, handles URL resolution and reverse URL lookups.
Utility functions and classes across various domains including datastructures, time zones, translation, logging, and functional programming helpers.
View and generic view implementations. Provides the controller pattern for handling HTTP requests and returning responses.

pipeline_trace.description: HTTP request enters the system as an HttpRequest object. Request parsing handles headers, query parameters, POST data, and file uploads. Middleware interception point established.

URL resolver matches the request path against URL patterns. Resolves to a view function or class-based view handler. Creates a URL resolution chain that may involve nested resolvers and captures route parameters.

View handler is called with HttpRequest and resolved URL parameters. View may be function-based or class-based (generic views). Handles business logic, form processing, and response generation. Calls ORM for data access.

View calls model methods or directly constructs QuerySet objects. Query translation transforms Python expressions to SQL. Connection management and transaction handling occur at this layer. Query execution returns model instances.

HTTP response is constructed (typically HttpResponse or subclass). Template rendering occurs if using template-based views. Response headers set and content type determined. Middleware chain runs in reverse order for response processing.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Core module is the foundation that many other modules depend on. It provides essential exception handling, configuration validation, cache infrastructure, and handler base classes that power the entire request/response cycle and are used by middleware, views, forms, and URL routing.

HTTP module sits at the boundary between Django and the web server. It provides the HttpRequest/HttpResponse abstractions used everywhere and contains middleware infrastructure that touches views, forms, and URL routing.
Utilities module provides general-purpose classes and functions used across the framework including datastructures, timezone handling, translation, and lazy evaluation patterns.

change_proposal.feature_summary: Add async-compatible structured logging middleware that captures per-request metadata (view name, ORM query count, response time) and emits it as structured log entries for observability and debugging.

new_types.name: StructuredLogMiddleware
new_types.description: Async-capable middleware that wraps request/response cycle to collect and emit metadata. Stores query count via thread-local or contextvar for async safety.

new_types.name: LogMetadata
new_types.description: Data class holding per-request metadata including view_name, query_count, start_time, path, status_code, and execution_time.

new_types.name: QueryCounter
new_types.description: Context manager or decorator that tracks database query count during view execution for accurate counting in complex scenarios.

new_types.name: LogEmitter
new_types.description: Instrumented logger adapter that formats structured JSON logs for output to logging framework or external services.

change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow existing Django middleware conventions (MiddlewareMixin class, sync/async handle methods, process_request/process_response pattern) as seen in common.py, csrf.py, and cache.py. Use Django's logging configuration from conf.settings.LOGGING and Python's standard logging module patterns.
change_proposal.integration_point: Hook into middleware stack at the beginning to start timing and metric collection, and at the end to emit structured logs. Integrates with Django's built-in logging configuration system via LOGGING setting. Follows WSGI/ASGI middleware conventions for Request/Response wrapping.
risks: Performance overhead: Additional CPU/time spent collecting metrics per-request, especially query counting which must be non-blocking and async-safe
Compatibility: Must maintain synchrony/asynchrony compatibility across Django's sync and async handlers and views
Maintainability: Additional complexity in middleware stack and potential for bugs in query counting across different ORM operations
Log verbosity: Risk of flooding logs with structured data if enabled project-wide without proper log level filtering
Thread-local/async var safety: Query counting must use contextvar for async views to avoid memory leaks and incorrect counts across await boundaries

tool_usage.params: repo root with summary=true to map top-level modules
django/django with max_depth=2 summary=true for core module structure
django/django/middleware with summary=true to identify middleware components
django/django/contrib with max_depth=1 to understand contrib modularity
django/django/apps/registry.py to extract app registry patterns
django/django/core/exceptions.py to identify core exception types
django/django/views with max_depth=1 for view module
